data.body:
SECURITY / OSS-ALIGNMENT ASSESSMENT (2026-07-13) — full write-up in session log quilted-resistant-waterbuck.

Benchmarked the shipped identity design (ADR 0044/0045) + this epic's plan against gittuf, TUF, Sigstore, in-toto/SLSA, git SSH-signing, RFC 8725, NIST SSDF (all cited).

VERDICT: strongly aligned with best practice — DSSE handling is textbook-conformant, the pinned per-kind scheme table is the structural RFC-8725 anti-alg-confusion defense, subject binding matches in-toto, and the commit-ancestry key-validity mechanism INDEPENDENTLY converged on gittuf's Reference-State-Log log-position model (NDSS 2025 Distinguished Paper). The op-cert 'trusted rebar-mcp fetches authoritative state' plan is exactly SLSA Build-L3's trusted-control-plane pattern. No fundamental redesign needed.

FOUR recommendations that touch THIS epic's scope (proposed, not yet ticketed):

R1 (HIGH — verify before high-authority use): ADD AN EXPLICIT ANTI-ROLLBACK/MONOTONICITY INVARIANT + regression test to the era-validity model. gittuf shipped the same log-position model and STILL had a rollback CVE (CVE-2026-44544) until it added a monotonic policy counter; TUF hard-codes N+1 root versioning. rebar's 'new event descends the revocation commit' appears to defeat the direct revoked-key-reuse case, BUT it rests on tickets-branch history integrity — and that branch is auto-pushed, NOT Gerrit-gated. Needs: (a) a test that a revoked-then-re-introduced key at a LATER ancestry position FAILS; (b) documented analysis of the tickets-branch force-push/rewrite threat (rebar's analog of gittuf's 'RSL push access'). The op-cert subject binding {ticket,material,merged-log-position} is the right place to anchor the monotonic position.

R2 (op-cert ADR): the HIGH-authority environment key has no independent transparency-log witness (the biggest divergence from Sigstore/Rekor). Prevention exists; third-party DETECTION of key misuse does not. Evaluate a bundled inclusion-proof / KEY_ADD monitoring as a 'Rekor-lite', and DOCUMENT why a full transparency log is out of scope. (Low-authority TOFU authorship is fine as-is.)

R3 (op-cert scope decision): no threshold/multi-sig — one compromised environment key can sign anything. TUF+gittuf use thresholds for high-authority roots. Either add threshold signing for required-environment keys, or ship a documented compromise/rotation runbook. Out-of-band pinned root partially mitigates.

R4 (docs, ADR 6a89): state explicitly that a valid op-cert attests state came from the trusted fetcher, NOT that the underlying work is correct (SLSA's own caveat) — so operators don't over-trust. Also pin cited spec versions/SHAs.

Awaiting direction on whether to file R1 (candidate bug/investigation ticket) and fold R2-R4 into children ee0b/8f1d/6a89.
